name: Oz
description: Oz platform for Linux server administration tasks: running shell commands, checking server health metrics, fetching application or Docker logs, deploying projects, and launching AI agents for infrastructure automation.
action_notes: CRITICAL: You MUST call the 'launch_agent' function for any infrastructure task that does not clearly match another action. NEVER return 'not_found'.
For greetings, social chat, or non-infrastructure questions → call greet
For quick bash/shell commands → call run_bash
For server CPU/memory/disk/service status → call server_health
For application or system log inspection → call app_logs
For Docker container logs and status → call docker_logs
For deploying a project (git pull, install, restart) → call deploy
For listing registered servers → call list_servers
For checking if a website is up/down, monitoring website status → call check_website
When a server name is mentioned, pass it as the server argument
DEFAULT RULE: For any infrastructure/sysadmin/DevOps/server query where no other function clearly matches → call launch_agent

greet.description: Handle greetings, social questions, and anything not related to servers or infrastructure. Use for: hello, hi, how are you, what can you do, who are you, thanks, goodbye.
utterance_samples: how are you
hello
hi there
what can you do
who are you
what are you
thank you
thanks
goodbye
good morning
what is your name

run_bash.description: Run a bash or shell command on this machine or a named server. Use for quick one-shot commands: docker ps, df -h, free -m, ls, curl, etc.

server.description: Target server name to run the command on. Empty means local machine.

utterance_samples: run docker ps
execute ls -la /var/www
check disk space with df -h
run docker ps -a on prod-web
check memory with free -m
list running processes
run uptime on the staging server
execute netstat -tlnp on db-server

server_health.description: Check the health of a server: CPU, memory, disk, load average, failed services, and Docker container status.

server.description: Target server name to check health on. Empty means local machine.

utterance_samples: check server health
server health check on prod-web
check health of the database server
show CPU and memory usage on prod
check system resources on app-server
disk space check on production server
check failed services on the server
server status report for staging
run health check on web server

app_logs.description: Retrieve and inspect application or system logs from a server. Supports log file paths, service names, and keyword filtering.

server.description: Target server name to fetch logs from. Empty means local machine.

path.description: Log file path or service name to inspect.

lines.description: Number of log lines to fetch.

utterance_samples: show application logs
fetch nginx error logs on prod-web
get last 200 lines of /var/log/app/error.log
show logs for the api service on staging
fetch logs on production filtered by ERROR
tail the app logs on the web server
show recent errors in the application logs
get last 50 lines from /var/log/syslog on db-server

docker_logs.description: Get Docker container logs and status. Can target a specific container or show all containers and their health.

server.description: Target server name for Docker operations. Empty means local machine.

lines.description: Number of log lines to fetch.

utterance_samples: show me docker logs for nginx
check the api container logs on prod-web
get last 100 lines of logs for the postgres container
why is the redis container crashing
show docker container status on staging
check all docker containers on the server
get logs from the web container with filter ERROR
is the nginx container running on production
how many docker containers are running on server AIT5252
how many containers are running on prod-web
list docker containers on staging
check docker containers on AIT5252
show running containers on the server
count docker containers on production

deploy.description: Deploy a project on a server: git pull the latest code, install dependencies, build, and restart the service.

server.description: Target server name to deploy to. Empty means local machine.

path.description: Project directory path on the server.

service.description: Service name to restart after deploy.

utterance_samples: deploy the project at /var/www/app on prod-web
deploy to production from the main branch
pull and restart the api service on staging
deploy /home/ubuntu/myapp to the web server
update and restart the nginx service on prod
deploy the app from branch develop on staging-server
run deployment for /opt/services/backend

launch_agent.description: FALLBACK FUNCTION: Call this function for any infrastructure/server/DevOps/sysadmin task that does not clearly match another action. Handles all server administration, troubleshooting, diagnostics, scripting, automation, configuration, package management, firewall setup, SSL renewal, monitoring, backups, user management, network troubleshooting, deployment, and any other infrastructure task expressed in natural language. When in doubt, call this function.

prompt.description: The full user request or task description to give to the agent.

utterance_samples: investigate why the API is returning 500 errors on prod
analyse the logs and find what is causing slowdowns
set up a cron job to back up the database daily
write a script to rotate the application logs
troubleshoot the failing deployment on staging
debug why the service keeps restarting on prod-web
diagnose the database connection issue on the server
install and configure nginx on the new server
set up a firewall with ufw on prod-web
renew SSL certificates on all servers
add a new user to the staging server
configure monitoring for the api service
set up daily database backups to S3
check why the network is slow on the database server
migrate the application from the old server to the new one
update all packages and reboot if necessary
set up a new docker compose stack for the staging environment
configure fail2ban on the production server
optimise the postgresql configuration for better performance
create a systemd service for the custom webhook handler
add swap space on the app server
set up logrotate configuration for nginx logs
configure automatic security updates
install nodejs and pm2 on the web server
set up a reverse proxy with nginx for the api
check the raid status and disk health on all servers
configure ssh key-based authentication for a new team member
set up a VPN connection between staging and production
run any infrastructure or server task
I need help with something on the server
can you handle a server task for me
help me with an infrastructure issue

check_website.description: Check if a website is up/down from server-side. Reports HTTP status code, response time, and any errors. Can optionally check from a specific server (external perspective).

url.description: The website URL to check (e.g. https://example.com).

domain.description: Alternative to url: the domain name to check (e.g. example.com).

server.description: Optional server to check FROM (external perspective). Empty means check from this machine.

utterance_samples: check if https://example.com is up
is my website https://mysite.com down
monitor https://api.myapp.com/health
check website status for https://shop.example.com
is https://blog.example.com reachable
check https://staging.example.com from the production server
verify https://app.example.com responds with 200

list_agents.description: List recent agent runs and their current status (completed, running, failed).
utterance_samples: list recent agent runs
show me the last agent tasks
what agents have been running
show agent history

get_agent.description: Get details, status and full output logs of a specific agent run by its ID.
utterance_samples: get status of agent 42
show output for agent run 7
what did agent 15 do
check on agent number 3

cancel_agent.description: Cancel a currently running agent by its ID.
utterance_samples: cancel agent 5
stop agent run 12
kill agent number 8

list_servers.description: List all registered servers in Oz, showing their names, hosts, and authentication type.
utterance_samples: list my servers
show registered servers
what servers do I have
show me all available servers
which servers are configured in Oz
